zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Booth Sheriff of Cheshire於1420年出生時，他的父親Sir Robert Booth38歲，母親Douce Venables24歲。他和Lady Maud  (Dutton) Brereton至少有1個兒子。他去世於1477年4月4日在英國爾鄧翰 梅西享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Booth Sheriff of Cheshire于1420年出生时，他的父亲Sir Robert Booth38岁，母亲Douce Venables24岁。他和Lady Maud  (Dutton) Brereton至少有1个儿子。他去世于1477年4月4日在英国爾鄧翰 梅西享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: Booth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): topographic or occupational name from Middle English bothe (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’. The latter sense was predominant in the Pennines of Lancashire and Yorkshire, where there were many cattle farms or vaccaries, and whose subdivisions were known as ‘booths’. The principal meaning of the surname there was therefore probably ‘cattle herdsman’, ‘man in charge of a vaccary’, and thus identical with Boothman . Elsewhere it may have denoted a shopkeeper who owned a temporary market stall, but no evidence has been found to confirm this use of the surname. In the British Isles the surname is still more common in northern England, where Scandinavian influence was more marked, and in Scotland, where the word was borrowed into Gaelic as both(an).
History: Robert Booth (1604–72) is mentioned in the colonial records of Exeter, NH, in 1645. He subsequently moved to ME.

variants.name: Boothman
meanings: English (northern): occupational name from Middle English both (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’ + man. In the Pennine uplands of northern England, where booths were subdivisons of vaccaries or cattle-rearing establishment, the name probably denoted a herdsman who looked after cattle, perhaps a man in charge of a vaccary. Elsewhere the surname might have denoted a shopkeeper but evidence for this lacking. Alternatively, the name can also be an occupational name for a servant of a man surnamed Booth .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Booth Sheriff of Cheshire was born in 1420, his father, Sir Robert Booth, was 38 and his mother, Douce Venables, was 24. He had at least 1 son with Lady Maud  (Dutton) Brereton. He died on 4 April 1477, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Booth Sheriff of Cheshire est né en 1420, son père, Sir Robert Booth, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Douce Venables, avait 24 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Lady Maud  (Dutton) Brereton. Il est décédé le 4 avril 1477, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Booth Sheriff of Cheshire 1420 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Robert Booth 38 und seine Mutter Douce Venables 24. Er hatte mit Lady Maud  (Dutton) Brereton mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 4. April 1477 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Booth Sheriff of Cheshire è nato il nel 1420, il suo padre, Sir Robert Booth, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Douce Venables, aveva 24 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Lady Maud  (Dutton) Brereton. Morì il 4 aprile 1477, nel Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Booth Sheriff of Cheshireが1420年に生まれた時，父Sir Robert Boothは38歳，母Douce Venablesは24歳であった。彼はLady Maud  (Dutton) Breretonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1477年4月4日にイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイで57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Booth Sheriff of Cheshire(이)가 1420년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert Booth(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Douce Venables(은)는 24세였다. 그는 Lady Maud  (Dutton) Brereton(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1477년 4월 4일 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Booth Sheriff of Cheshire nasceu em 1420, seu pai, Sir Robert Booth, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Douce Venables, tinha 24 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Lady Maud  (Dutton) Brereton. Ele faleceu em 4 de abril de 1477, em Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Booth Sheriff of Cheshire родился в 1420 г., его отцу по имени Sir Robert Booth было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Douce Venables, 24. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Lady Maud  (Dutton) Brereton. Он умер 4 апреля 1477 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Booth Sheriff of Cheshire nació en 1420, su papá, Sir Robert Booth, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Douce Venables, tenía 24 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Lady Maud  (Dutton) Brereton. Murió el 4 de abril de 1477, en Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 57 años.
